methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
KrattWorks is a Series A–stage defense and security company founded by Martin Karmin, with reported revenue of €19.7M. The valuation assessment draws from a universe of 10 comparable companies across the defense and security sector—2 public peers and 8 private equivalents—including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Anduril Industries.

The peer-median approach, applied across four same-sector, same-stage comparables, yields a median valuation of $400M. The peer set ranges from $150M (Ares Industries) to $29.0B (Scale AI), reflecting substantial dispersion typical of frontier-tech defense valuations. This wide range reflects heterogeneous business models and market positioning within the sector. The median of $400M represents the central tendency across the comparable group and serves as the primary anchor for valuation.

The triangulated analysis, constrained by single-method applicability, supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with a median point of $400M. Key uncertainties include the relevance of Scale AI's outlier $29.0B valuation to KrattWorks' positioning, the durability of revenue-based comparisons absent profitability data, and stage-specific risk differentials among the peer set. Revenue efficiency and path to scale within government procurement cycles represent material valuation drivers not fully captured in static peer multiples.
